channel.title: Mesopotamia on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Mesopotamia on RaihnForge

item.title: First Cities — From Gobekli Tepe to Ur, the Bargain of Civilization
item.description:
The story of the first cities is the story of the bargain humanity struck when it stopped moving. The bargain is a real one, with real costs, and the early sites preserve the moment-by-moment record of who paid and who collected.

Gobekli Tepe: A Meeting Place Before a City

The ancient city of Gobekli Tepe is thought to be a gathering place of hunters and gatherers in order to conduct rituals and feasts. It was important for migratory groups to meet occasionally in order to exchange information and goods, as well as to find marriage partners to broaden the gene pool.

item.title: Code of Hammurabi — Reading a Mesopotamian Society Through Its Laws
item.description:
The Code of Hammurabi reads less like a list of rules and more like a cross-section of a society. The punishments are not interchangeable. Who you injured, and what class they belonged to, decided what you owed. Reading the code as a primary source means letting the price list do the talking.

Social Hierarchy: Reading the Price List

There seems to be a patriarchal class social hierarchy based on the Code of Hammurabi. Men, women, plebeian status, or slave status are often noted in the punishments of violations. For instance:
